description: Summary of AI agent responses to incoming PR comments. Shows each reviewer comment paired with the AI's draft reply and/or proposed code fix. Per-response actions let you approve/delete individual replies and approve/reject individual code fix proposals. Entry-level actions support bulk approve, submit replies, apply proposals, and cleanup.

contentTemplate.label: Pull Request
contentTemplate.description: PR metadata. Expected keys: Repository, Branch (source -> target), Author, PR URL.

contentTemplate.label: Response Summary
contentTemplate.description: Overview of how the AI responded. Expected columns: Action, Count. Rows: Replies, Code Fixes, Won't Fix, Total Comments Addressed.

contentTemplate.title: Comment Responses
contentTemplate.description: Each nested section represents one incoming reviewer comment and the AI's response to it. Contains the original comment, the AI's draft reply, an optional proposed code fix diff, and per-response actions.

contentTemplate.label: Details
contentTemplate.description: Response metadata. Expected keys: Thread ID, Commenter (who left the original comment), File (relative path), Lines (line range), Action (Reply / Code Fix / Won't Fix), Agent (AI agent name), Reply Draft ID (PowerReview draft UUID), Proposal ID (PowerReview proposal UUID, if code fix).

contentTemplate.label: Original Comment
contentTemplate.description: The reviewer's original comment that the AI is responding to.

contentTemplate.label: AI Response
contentTemplate.description: The AI's draft reply text. This is submitted as a reply on the comment thread when approved.

contentTemplate.label: Proposed Fix
contentTemplate.description: The unified diff of the proposed code fix. Only present when the AI created a code change proposal. Use language 'diff'.

expectedActions.label: Approve Reply
expectedActions.description: Approve the draft reply. CLI: powerreview comment approve --pr-url <url> --draft-id <id>

expectedActions.label: Delete Reply
expectedActions.description: Delete the draft reply. CLI: powerreview comment delete --pr-url <url> --draft-id <id>

expectedActions.label: Approve Proposal
expectedActions.description: Approve the proposed code fix. CLI: powerreview proposal approve --pr-url <url> --proposal-id <id>

expectedActions.label: Reject Proposal
expectedActions.description: Reject the proposed code fix. CLI: powerreview proposal reject --pr-url <url> --proposal-id <id>

contentTemplate.title: Proposed Code Fixes
contentTemplate.description: Optional dedicated section listing all proposed code fixes with their diffs. Useful when the user wants to review all code changes together rather than inline with each comment.

contentTemplate.label: Summary
contentTemplate.description: Overall summary of what the AI did: how many comments were addressed, what actions were taken, any comments that could not be addressed.

contentTemplate.label: View PR
contentTemplate.description: Link to the pull request on Azure DevOps or GitHub.

expectedActions.label: Open PR
expectedActions.description: Open the pull request in the browser.

expectedActions.label: Approve All Replies
expectedActions.description: Approve all draft replies. CLI: powerreview comment approve-all --pr-url <url>

expectedActions.label: Submit Replies
expectedActions.description: Submit all approved replies to the remote provider. CLI: powerreview submit --pr-url <url>

expectedActions.label: Approve All Proposals
expectedActions.description: Approve all proposed code fixes. Requires per-proposal approval via CLI.

expectedActions.label: Apply All Proposals
expectedActions.description: Apply all approved proposals (cherry-pick into PR branch and push). Requires proposals to be approved first.

expectedActions.label: Delete All
expectedActions.description: Delete all AI draft replies and reject all proposals.
